description: Buffed Clodsire lead (improved Earthquake) with a soft Trailblaze Furret safe swap and an Abomasnow closer whose faster Energy Ball makes it a strong answer to water types and a closer pacing match against Feraligatr.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

performanceNotes: Clodsire benefits from the Earth Power/Earthquake buff this season
Abomasnow's buffed (faster) Energy Ball improves it vs water types
Cradily is a cited alternative safe swap to Furret

description: A glass-cannon Sunshine Cup team led by Blaziken with Blast Burn (legacy) and Aura Sphere, backed by a spammy Toucannon safe swap and a Quagsire closer. The back two cover each other's weaknesses while Blaziken applies heavy spam pressure.
performanceNotes: Blaziken is extremely spammy and hits hard with Ember/Blast Burn/Aura Sphere, punishing the normal-typing-heavy meta
Toucannon acts as a glassy but very spammy safe swap that can roam free thanks to few steels/pure rocks in the meta
Quagsire's fast Aqua Tail pacing overpowers Garchomp, Gligar and other matchups; creator notes Shadow Blaziken (with Counter) would be even stronger

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: BLAZIKEN: The Glass Cannon Tearing Through Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO

performanceNotes: Coalossal is very hard to remove from the field due to its bulk
Furret's charged-move choice flexes coverage between ground and normal threats

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

description: Simple Toucannon lead that handles fighting types, with a Furret safe swap (can run Trailblaze for ground types or Brick Break for normal types) and a bulky Coalossal closer to clean up what Toucannon doesn't want to face.
Double-grass Sunshine Cup team with Coalossal lead and Gogoat in the back, using Ludicolo as the safe swap. Coalossal runs Flame Charge for the attack boost alongside Rock Slide.
performanceNotes: Initial version played in the first ~5 battles
Coalossal is the #2 ranked Pokémon in Sunshine Cup
Flame Charge ramps attack but creator felt Rock Slide's better damage per energy should be used more

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: COALOSSAL brings HUGE BULK to Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Revised Sunshine Cup team swapping Ludicolo for Miltank as the safe swap to better handle normal/fighting types in the meta, keeping Coalossal lead and Gogoat closer.
performanceNotes: Switched to Miltank after running into normal/fighting types
Miltank felt to have more overall play in the meta despite being weak to normal/fighting
Creator was 107-60 (~64% win rate) at rank 18 this season

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: COALOSSAL brings HUGE BULK to Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: An 'all safe' Sunshine Cup team led by Swampert with Cradily and Ferrothorn as safe swaps. Swampert is only weak to grass, and both backline Pokémon answer grass while having broadly safe matchups; the creator sometimes stands with Swampert to out-spam with Hydro Cannon then pivots to Cradily as a half-sacrifice.
performanceNotes: Creator went 7-3 with this team
Swampert only weak to grass, with two grass answers in the back
Cradily and Ferrothorn both function as reliable safe swaps in this limited meta

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: SWAMPERT Makes Sunshine Cup EASY | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

performanceNotes: Confusion was buffed and is unresisted in this cup, making Oranguru a strong spam pivot
Run Magcargo with Ember instead of Incinerate for faster energy
Cradily uses Acid + Octazooka + Grass Knot rather than the standard set
description: Cradily lead with Oranguru as a confusion-spamming pivot and Magcargo as the closer. Built around buffing confusion (which nothing resists in this cup) and Trail Blaze boosts.

sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Sunshine Cup | Pokemon GO PvP
sources.author: YasserPoGO

description: Grass-type Cradily lead paired with a Ninetales running Energy Ball to bait and punish ground types, with Oranguru in the back to cover Cradily's weaknesses.
performanceNotes: Energy Ball Ninetales is the best answer to the Ninetales mirror and baits ground types
Oranguru pairs really well with Cradily's weaknesses
Lots of flexible alternatives exist for the back slot

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

description: Toucannon and Swampert paired to cover each other's weaknesses — Swampert (a 'mud boy' only weak to grass) is backed by flyer Toucannon to handle grass, with Cradily as a safe swap providing rock coverage for fires and grass for neutral situations.
performanceNotes: Toucannon and Swampert cover each other's weaknesses well
Cradily safe swap is broadly safe with rock coverage for fires
Swampert handles Coalossal which Toucannon loses to

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: BEST SUNSHINE CUP TEAMS | BEST RANKED POKEMON | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Gligar lead with excellent meta typing, a buffed Drill Run Donphan, and an Oranguru closer that is nearly impossible to hard-counter.
performanceNotes: Gligar's typing and Night Slash make it a strong, bulky answer to Oranguru mirrors
Donphan's Drill Run was buffed this season
Oranguru has next to no hard answers in the meta

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

description: Piloswine lead acts as a core-breaker against flying and ground types that few people prepare for, backed by a Fearow safe swap with Drill Run ground coverage and a Tornadus closer to patch tricky matchups.
performanceNotes: Piloswine's ice typing is under-prepared-for and breaks common cores
Fearow's Drill Run punishes ground types that swap in
Tornadus is strong into the common Oranguru

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: The BEST Teams to CLIMB for SUNSHINE CUP in Pokemon GO!

description: Kanto Ninetales lead with a double Mud Boy back of Seismitoad and Quagsire. Ninetales handles grass and plays out matchups while the ground-types provide safe, bulky coverage; Seismitoad is the safe swap with Icy Wind for grass.
performanceNotes: Creator went roughly 8-1/8-2 with the team
Ninetales runs Overheat over Energy Ball as a surprise closing nuke
Double Mud Boy gives very safe, bulky matchups in the meta with grass covered by Ninetales/Seismitoad

sources.videoTitle: The MUDBOYS RULE the Sunshine Cup | Sunshine Cup Team  | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: A balanced Pidgeot / Seismitoad / Quagsire team the creator has been running successfully across Great and Ultra League, considered one of his best.
performanceNotes: Creator rates it among the best teams he uses across multiple formats
Can struggle slightly into the meta's ice types
